judgment.summary: Strong recent earnings growth and solid margins are tempered by a high valuation and significant debt load.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last quarter beat EPS estimate by 1.2%; next earnings date is 2026-07-28.
positives: Quarterly earnings growth 136.3% YoY and Earnings growth 138.9% YoY indicate strong recent performance.
Revenue growth 20.0% YoY shows healthy top-line expansion.
Profit margin 11.1% and Operating margin 15.7% are respectable.
Analyst consensus is a buy with a mean target of $197.67.
negatives: Trailing P/E of 88.67 and Forward P/E of 43.67 are very high, suggesting the stock is richly valued.
Total debt $9.92B vs Total cash $1.76B indicates substantial leverage.
Debt/Equity ratio of 80.36% is on the higher side.
Quick ratio of 0.75 suggests potential short-term liquidity constraints.

news.paragraph: Corning (GLW) is seeing mixed signals from Wall Street, with one analyst raising their price target while Jim Cramer expressed a lack of buying interest despite recent price weakness. This divergence suggests potential disagreement on the stock's valuation and future prospects. Investors should consider the upcoming earnings report on July 28th for further clarity on the company's performance and outlook.

headlines.title: Jim Cramer on Corning: “The Beatdown’s Not Enough to Entice Me to Buy”
Mizuho Raises its Price Target on Corning (GLW)
